title: Does Masseter Toxin Help With Jawline Filler Results？ | Dr Tim's Tips

excerpt: So does master reduction help with jawline filler results [Music] they're actually kind of working in opposite ways so a dermal filler is usually could be lifting the jawline and then creating more shape but often it's injected straight into the area where the master is or at lea…

segments.text: so does master reduction help with jawline filler results [Music] they're actually kind of working in opposite ways so a dermal filler is usually could be lifting the jawline and then creating more shape but often it's injected straight into the area where the master is or at least slightly posterior to it under the chin and you're creating more definitions so they're often working in different ways because master reduction is shrinking the area that you're actually defining with dermal fillers so i'd say in most cases you're unlikely to be doing both or you've kind of taken away the master and then added the definition with dermal filler but there are reasons when that could really be helpful so if you have a particularly full face with not very much definition posteriorly you could be adding definition or crispness to the jawline while decreasing the overall volume of it it's possible but it'd be quite a sophisticated treatment done in stages i think most commonly with jaw jawline

is you're hopefully treating someone who just needs definitional shape it works best with kind of 35 year olds who are just losing some of the contours and you just restore them naturally i think when you're reducing the master and then trying to add it back and you're getting quite complicated so i would very rarely combine that as a treatment and so your average result i would your average treatment i'd say would be one or the other and not both

transcript: so does master reduction help with jawline filler results [Music] they're actually kind of working in opposite ways so a dermal filler is usually could be lifting the jawline and then creating more shape but often it's injected straight into the area where the master is or at least slightly posterior to it under the chin and you're creating more definitions so they're often working in different ways because master reduction is shrinking the area that you're actually defining with dermal fillers so i'd say in most cases you're unlikely to be doing both or you've kind of taken away the master and then added the definition with dermal filler but there are reasons when that could really be helpful so if you have a particularly full face with not very much definition posteriorly you could be adding definition or crispness to the jawline while decreasing the overall volume of it it's possible but it'd be quite a sophisticated treatment done in stages i think most commonly with jaw jawline is you're hopefully treating someone who just needs definitional shape it works best with kind of 35 year olds who are just losing some of the contours and you just restore them naturally i think when you're reducing the master and then trying to add it back and you're getting quite complicated so i would very rarely combine that as a treatment and so your average result i would your average treatment i'd say would be one or the other and not both
